title: Expression	and	purification	of	PPM1H phosphatase
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"asil7","text":"A subset of small GTPases of the Rab family including Rab8A (Uniprot: P61006) and Rab10 (Uniprot: P61026) have been identified as substrates of the Leucine Rich Repeat Kinase 2 (LRRK2; Uniprot Q5S007)) (Steger et al., 2016). Recent work has revealed that Protein Phosphatase PPM1H (Uniprot Q9ULR3) is a cellular phosphatase that efficiently dephosphorylates LRRK2 phosphorylated Rab proteins (Berndsen et al., 2019). In order to study in detail the kinetics, mechanism and structure of PPM1H, it is necessary to produce milligrams of pure PPM1H protein. Here we describe in detail the method we use to produce milligram quantities PPM1H and several mutants.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Mix 10 µL of pET15b 6HIS-SUMO-PPM1H plasmid (around 50 ng/µl ) with 50 µL - 100 µL of the competent BL21(DE3) cells and incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .
Transfer the vial to a heat block equilibrated at 42 °C and leave for 00:00:50 .
Transfer the vial back into ice and add 1 mL SOC medium and mix gently.
Incubate for 04:00:00 at 37 °C for recovery.
Plate 0.1 mL of the transformation onto a LB broth/ agar plate supplemented with 50 µg/ml Carbenicillin.
Leave the plate Overnight in a 37 °C incubator.
Using a 200 µL sterile pipette tip, remove one of the colonies from the plate and drop the tip into 100 mL LB broth medium, supplemented with 50 mg/L Carbenicillin.
Incubate at 37 °C with 180 rpm, °C, - 200 rpm, °C, rotational shaking Overnight . The culture medium should become totally opaque in the morning.
Decant 3 x 1 L LB broth medium into 3 x 2 L conical flasks.
Supplement each flask / litre with 1 mL of 50 mg/mL Carbenicillin.
Mix and add 10 mL - 25 mL of the Overnight culture into each flask.
Incubate for 04:00:00 at 37 °C , using an Infors Shaker-Incubator set at 200 rpm, °C, .
Sample two or three of the expressions by removing 1 mL medium and comparing the optical density at 600 nm with fresh LB medium, using a WPA cell densitometer or a spectrometer.
When the OD 600 has reached 0.7-0.9, change the temperature setting of the Infors incubator to 16 °C and incubate the cells for at least another hour, all the while shaking at 200 rpm, °C, .

When the flasks have cooled down to 20 °C or lower, induce PPM1H expression by supplementing the medium with 0.025 millimolar (mM) IPTG. This is achieved by adding 25 µL of a 1 Molarity (M) IPTG stock solution to each litre.
Leave the cells to express the protein for Overnight at 16 °C .
The following morning prepare 0.5 L of cell collection buffer and chill On ice .

Decant the content of the 2 L conical flasks into 1 L Beckman centrifuge pots, close the pots with their screw-cap lids and sediment the cells by centrifugation for 00:25:00 at 4 °C at 4200 rpm, °C, using a Beckman J6 centrifuge with the 6 x 1 L rotor (4.2).

Collect and open the pots and carefully decant the spent supernatant medium back into the flasks. The flasks can now be sent for cleaning and autoclaving. The cell sediment in the pots is expected to have a volume of 3 mL – 5 mL .
Add 18 mL of cold cell collection buffer to the sediment.
Transfer the 1 L pots to the Infors incubator and set the temperature to 14 °C and the rotation to 110 rpm, °C, .
Leave the pots for 00:30:00 , after which time the cell sediment should have completely resuspended.

Pool all suspensions into one of the pots using a 25 mL pipette and a good pipettor. If any of the sediments has not well resuspended, pipette up and down close to the bottom of the pots. It is very useful at this stage to supplement the NaCl concentration to 250 millimolar (mM) and add glycerol to 5% (by vol) final concentration. It is important to add the NaCl and glycerol after the resuspension step and not before as this interferes with the resuspension.
Once the NaCl and glycerol has been mixed in, 45 mL samples are aliquoted into 50 mL centrifuge vials and freeze them in liquid nitrogen for at least 00:20:00 .
Store the vials at -20 °C . The freezing and subsequent thawing step breaks up the cells and improves yield. Vials can be stored at -20ºC for up to 4 weeks.
Slowly thaw the vials with the cell suspension in cold water.
After thawing chill suspension On ice and then sonicate, using a probe sonicator (Cell disruptor). Settings: 6 – 8 pulses of 00:00:15 with 00:00:15 pauses. Set the amplitude to 50%.

Transfer the sonicated suspension into 50 mL Beckman centrifuge vials and sediment the insoluble material by centrifugation for 00:25:00 at 40000 x g, °C, and 4 °C using a JA25.50 or a JA30.50 rotor in a Beckman Avanti centrifuge.

Recover the supernatant by carefully decanting it into 50 mL conical centrifuge tubes. During the centrifugation step equilibrate 3.0 mL Cobalt-NTA-resin ( 6 mL of a 50% slurry by vol, sufficient for a 3 L expression) by washing it three times with Milli Q water and once with cell collection buffer.

Add a 1 mL of a 50% slurry (by vol) of the washed Cobalt-NTA-resin in collection buffer to each 50 mL vial with the lysate and incubate the mix on a Roller Mixer for 01:30:00 in a cold room set at 4 °C .
Avoid excessive agitation and especially formation of foam.
In the meanwhile, prepare the washbuffer.
Sediment the Cobalt-NTA-resin by centrifugation at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 at 4 °C using a Eppendorf R5810.
Remove the lid and carefully remove the supernatant containing the depleted lysate using a 10 mL pipette, being careful not to disturb the resin.
Add 3 mL of wash buffer. Prepare a 1000 µL pipette tip by removing 5 mm – 7 mm from the pointed end using scissors. This allows it to be used to facilely resuspend the agarose.
Resuspend the Cobalt-NTA-resin using a P1000 with such a modified blue tip and combine the resin into a 15 mL centrifuge vial.
Wash out any remaining agarose from the large vials with 1 mL of wash buffer and pool with the first batch to maximise recovery.
Fill the 15 mL vial to the top with wash buffer, mix well and sediment resin by centrifugation at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 using an Eppendorf 5810 R centrifuge.
Remove the wash buffer with a thin vacuum line and replace with fresh wash buffer. Repeat this step 5 times in total to thoroughly wash the resin.
Remove all wash buffer without disturbing the agarose bed.
Resuspend the resin into 3 mL wash buffer, transfer into a BIORAD Polyprep column and let the buffer run out.
Add 1 mL of elution buffer and let the void of the bed run out.
Keep the sample volume small to avoid unnecessary protein concentration steps before the subsequent size exclusion.
Then elute the protein with 5 mL elution buffer into a 15 mL centrifuge vial. This should provide 5 mL of protein solution of 3.0 mg/mL - 5.0 mg/mL ( 9 mg - 15 mg total protein).

For certain applications it may be desirable to remove the N-terminal 6His-SUMO-tag. This can be achieved by incubation of the eluted PPM1H with His-SENP1 (415-644). For each 10 mg of eluted PPM1H, mix with 1 mg of His-SENP1.

Transfer the proteins into a Snake Skin dialysis tube and dialyse Overnight against 5 L of dialysis buffer. Dialysis removes 95% of the imidazole and also concentrates the PPM1H, due to the higher glycerol concentration in the dialysis buffer.
Recover the protein the next day and remove the 6-His-SUMO-tag and the His-SENP1 by letting the proteins drip through a 2 mL bed of Cobalt-NTA-resin, equilibrate in dialysis buffer.
Wash the bed with 2 mL of dialysis buffer and pool this wash with the flow through to maximise recovery.
In order to further improve the purity of the PPM1H and to separate the active dimer from less active tetramer, employ SEC using a SD200 column.
Equilibrate a Superdex 200 XK 16/60 format in 3 column volumes of SEC buffer at a flowrate of 1.2 ml/min using an Äkta Purifier or an Äkta Pure.
Apply 1.5 mL aliquots of the PPM1H sample (typical concentration of 3.0 mg/mL - 4.5 mg/mL ) to the column at a flowrate of 1.2 ml/min and collect 1.2 mL fractions between 44 mL (void) to 110 mL .
Elute the tetrameric PPM1H as a shoulder between 60 mL and 75 mL .
The dimeric PPM1H elutes as a peak between 75 mL and 87 mL ( apex at 81 mL ). We do not observe a monomer of PPM1H.
Pool and concentrate the fractions from 77 mL to 87 mL to obtain the dimeric PPM1H. A typical elution chromatogram for PPM1H is shown on Figure 1a and for PPM1H (33-end).
Separate 3 µg of the protein on a Bis-Tris 4% - 12% SDS-polyacrylamide gel and stain with Instant Blue Figure 2.
creator: Dario R Alessi
